title: Brassaia sprucei
taxonomy: Brassaia sprucei is a member of the family Proteaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is also known as the cabbage tree or tī kōuka. The classification of this plant can be broken down further into its kingdom (Plantae), phylum (Angiospermophyta), class (Magnoliopsida), and order (Proteales). As a member of the Proteaceae family, Brassaia sprucei is closely related to other plants such as Banksia and Grevillea. Its classification is an important part of understanding its evolutionary history and its place within the plant kingdom.

side_effects: Brassaia sprucei, also known as the Takhia tree, is native to Borneo. There is limited information available regarding the side effects of Brassaia sprucei.
